target.name: Roam Robotics
target.founder: Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, Saul Griffith

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Roam Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $16.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 7 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, ranging from $300M to $10.0B in valuation, alongside 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $16M in total raised and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage step-ups, implies a valuation of $33M. Peer-median analysis across the 7-company robotics cohort—including Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M, Series B)—yields a median valuation of $1.6B.

The wide dispersion between methods reflects material uncertainty in the peer set. The stage-progression result of $33M reflects capital deployed; the peer-median of $1.6B reflects market valuations of later-stage robotics companies. Triangulating these methods produces a range of $28M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $841M. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-adjusted discount relative to Series B peers and the degree to which later-stage valuations in this sector are sustainable given current deployment and revenue profiles.
